title: rhamnus alaternus subsp. picenensis
intro: Rhamnus alaternus subsp. picenensis, also known as Picene alaterno, is a subspecies of the Italian buckthorn, a deciduous shrub native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of Rhamnus alaternus subsp. picenensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used in capsules or powder form.
